learning_outcomes.description: Apply knowledge of the anatomical structures of the orofacial region to the treatment of fully edentulous mouths.
Demonstrate knowledge of and familiarity with the laboratory skills and procedures required for practice of removable prosthodontics procedures.
Apply scientific principles of radiation and radiation safety to the process of taking intra-oral radiographs in a simulated environment.
Identify the radiographic features of structures of the orofacial region.
Develop interprofessional practice skills including role clarification, team functioning, interprofessional communication and client-centred care.

assessments.due_date:
10/08/2026 - 21/09/2026

 Assessment session/date depends on group allocation available on Blackboard
assessments.task_description:
This is a MUST PASS assessment item.

• Each student will participate fully in a 3-hour session, along with 6-7 of their peers

• Each student will practise taking intraoral radiographs in a simulated environment on a manikin.

• The report generated by the session will be sent to the year 2 coordinator to indicate student has completed radiology practical requirements in preparation for clinic in Year 3. Attendance is mandatory for this activity as it is a part of a required course activity in addition to completing the course successfully.

• Students must demonstrate competency in taking intra-oral radiographs at the end of the assigned session in Semester 2.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Will be submitted from the Radiology clinic once session is completed.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: Absences to be reported as make up sessions may not be possible.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date:
24/09/2026 9:00 am

Your specific examination time will depend on your group allocation which you will be informed of via Blackboard.
assessments.task_description:
The Observed Structured Clinical Examination will be conducted in Week 9. 

Students will work through stations where you will be asked to answer questions related to removable prosthodontics tasks that you have been taught in the preclinic through the semester. 

Tasks can include but not limited to: adapting light cured acrylic and making a special tray, identifying errors in a denture teeth set up, identifying equipment and demonstrating their use (Eg articulator, fox plane, trimmer, green stick compound, wax knife), identifying anatomical landmarks and significance in edentulous casts. 

Marks are awarded for successfully performing composite tasks at each station.

Example stations will be made available to students.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The examination paper will be submitted to invigilators on completion of the exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.category: Notebook/ Logbook, Performance, Practical/ Demonstration, Reflection
assessments.task_description:
The purpose of this assessment is to evaluate the student’s pre-clinical/peer clinic performance at the interim stage and provide formative feedback to support improvement before the final clinical competence assessment at the end of semester.

Course Coordinator in consultation with the pre-clinical/peer clinic supervisory team will complete a report based on student’s clinical performance during the assessment period and quality of critical self-reflections following each session.

See Submission Guidelines for all components that must be submitted for the interim clinical practice performance task to be assessed. Detailed assessment criteria and rubrics will be provided on Blackboard.

Two domains will be assessed to an expectations met (EM) or not met (ENM) standard and mapped to the Professional competency requirements for the accreditation body for this program - The Australian Dental Council (Professional Competencies of the newly Qualified Dentist)

The domains being assessed are: 

1. Professionalism, Communication and Health Promotion 

2. Clinical Treatment and Evaluation

To achieve a pass, the criteria are as follows:

All domains are weighted equally, and the student must achieve a pass in each domain, and an overall pass.

  • A pass in each domain will involve a student receiving 3 or fewer "expectations not met" (ENM) mark in Domain 1, and no more than 3 "expectation not met" (ENM) in Domains 2. 
  • An overall pass for the report will involve a student receiving 6 or fewer "expectations not met" (ENM) mark across the 2 domains.


Please note:

  • Written feedback will be provided for any areas to improve as this is formative. Students must reflect on feedback and demonstrate improvement in the latter half of the semester.
  • Logbooks may be randomly audited by the Course Coordinator for authenticity and supervisor signatures.
  • Any significant clinical concerns related to safety will be escalated immediately via course coordinator pathways and documented and taken into consideration for the assessment outcomes.


There is no resubmission or resit available for this component. This is a formative assessment to enable the student to receive feedback on their developing clinical competency.

Any significant clinical concerns pertaining to patient safety will be escalated via clinical communication pathways to the course coordinator and recorded on this report to be considered for the assessment outcome.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The submitted portfolio must be COMPLETE in all the following respects and submitted by the published due date which will then be assessed as part of this assessment task. Any incomplete or missing information can result in a fail grade in this must pass assessment task.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The purpose of this assessment is to evaluate the student’s pre-clinical and peer clinic performance for this course, at the end of the semester. Course Coordinator in consultation with the pre-clinical/peer clinic supervisory team will complete a report based on student’s pre-clinical /peer clinic performance during the assessment period and quality of critical self-reflections following each session.

Students are advised to review any feedback provided in the interim report, to improve pre-clinical/peer clinic performance, in the latter part of his course, to pass this final clinical practice performance assessment which is must pass to pass this course.

Detailed marking rubric for this assessment is available on Blackboard.

See Submission Guidelines for all components that must be submitted for this clinical practice performance task to be assessed. The submitted portfolio will be reviewed and performance in each domain considered and used to complete a report of overall performance in each domain as per the criteria and standards.

The clinical portfolio will be reviewed and performance in each domain considered and used to complete a report of overall performance in each domain.

Two domains will be assessed to an expectations met (EM) or not met (ENM) standard and mapped to the professional competency requirements for the accreditation body for this program - The Australian Dental Council (Professional Competencies of the newly Qualified Dentist)

The domains being assessed are: 

1. Professionalism, Communication and Health Promotion 

2. Clinical Treatment and Evaluation

Passing requirements:

To pass this assessment, students must achieve a pass on this summative assessment on clinical performance progress at this stage. To achieve a pass, the criteria are as follows:

All domains are weighted equally, and the student must achieve a pass in each domain, and an overall pass.

  • A pass in each domain will involve a student receiving 3 or fewer "expectations not met" (ENM) mark in Domain 1, and no more than 3 "expectation not met" (ENM) in Domain 2. 
  • An overall pass for the report will involve a student receiving 6 or fewer "expectations not met" (ENM) mark across the 2 domains.


Students must complete all required components as outlined in Submission Guidelines for the portfolio submission.

Please note:

  • Written feedback provided for any areas to improve from the interim task, must be reflected on and student should demonstrate improvement in the latter half of the semester to enable passing of this must pass task.
  • Logbooks may be randomly audited by the Course Coordinator for authenticity and supervisor signatures.
  • Any significant clinical concerns related to safety will be escalated immediately via course coordinator communication pathways and documented in the report and taken into consideration for the final assessment outcome.


To pass this assessment item, students must actively participate in a minimum of 90% of pre-clinic sessions and 90% of peer-clinic sessions as per published rosters and timetables. All allocated tasks in each session must be completed successfully and allocated clinical educator must deem them to be satisfactory.

In accordance with PPL Assessment Procedure Section 3 Part J, supplementary assessment will not be available for this final clinical practice performance task, as professional competencies are developed over a 8-week practicum. There is no resubmission or resit option available for this assessment either.ﾠ
assessments.submission_guidelines: The submitted portfolio must be COMPLETE in all the following respects and submitted by the published due date which will then be assessed as part of this assessment task. Any incomplete or missing information can result in a fail grade in this must pass assessment task.

assessments.due_date: 28 September to 29 October 2026 (depends on group allocation; 2 weeks after last tutorial in each group)
assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Essay/ Critique, Presentation
assessments.task_description:
Please refer to the assessment information on Learn.UQ for the assessment due date for your team.

This is a team assessment, and all team members are expected to contribute equally to the team’s assessment items. Teams will be assigned during your first tutorial. There are three (3) parts to this assessment:

Part One requires you to complete a worksheet and will draw on content from Module 1 of the IPCP curriculum focusing on role-clarification and negotiation, and relationship focused care and services. Length: There is no word limit for the worksheet.

Part Two involves creating a 3-minute video which draws upon the content from Modules 2 and 3 of the IPCP curriculum focusing on team communication, team functioning and relationship focused care and services. Length: Video 3 minutes. 

Part Three involves a check-in with a tutor, to assess and reflect on processes of collaboration

Length: approx. 10 minutes.

 Teams will need to submit Parts One and Two of your assessment in the Assessment tab on the HMBS1240 IPCP Learn.UQ course site. Additional information regarding submission will be provided on the HMBS1240 IPCP Learn.UQ course site.

Please refer to the assessment information on Learn.UQ for further details and requirements for the assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: This exam will be held in-person via Inspera in the scheduled end of semester examination block. The exam will be for 90 mins duration. Please arrive 15 mins early to allow for ID check and set up in Inspera. Students will need a laptop for this exam with Inspera safe browser installed. If you do not have a laptop with access to the internet (connect to Eduroam) - you may be able to borrow a laptop from the Library (https://web.library.uq.edu.au/library-and-student-it-help/using-library-devices/laptop-loans). The exam may contain a mixture of multiple choice questions (MCQ), problem solving such as match the following, fill in the blanks, drag and drop labels, and/or short answer questions (SAQ). The exam will be worth 90 marks in total. It will cover content from learning activities in weeks 1-12 inclusive.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Your exam will be submitted automatically at the completion of your examination time.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 9(27 Jul - 27 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Preclinic Laboratory

Students will learn the laboratory process of creating Full Dentures from initially constructing special trays on edentulous models to creating occlusal rims and setting teeth to partially waxing up a full denture.
learning_activities.topic_description: Preclinic Laboratory Students will learn the laboratory process of creating Full Dentures  from initially constructing special trays on edentulous models to creating occlusal rims and setting teeth to partially waxing up a full denture.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Clinical Lectures

Clinical instructions on the treatment and management of the edentulous patient in order to create a set of functional, aesthetic removable dentures.

Clincial coaching on radiographic anatomy and dental radiograph interpretation.
learning_activities.topic_description: Clinical Lectures Clinical instructions on the treatment and management of the edentulous patient in order to create a set of functional, aesthetic removable dentures. Clincial coaching on radiographic anatomy and dental radiograph interpretation.

learning_activities.topic:
Interprofessional Collaborative Practice Modules

Interprofessional Collaborative Practice Modules (Monday pm/ Thursday am) (Tutorials); Dates on Allocate +

Participants will work in small teams in tutorial groups with students from Dentistry, Exercise Physiology and Sports Science, Midwifery, Nursing, Occupational Therapy, Pharmacy, Physiotherapy, Social Work and Speech Pathology.

There are three modules that consist of on-line preparation and face-to-face learning activities. You will work in your team to engage in a range of learning activities to develop your knowledge and skills to help prepare you for working in teams when caring for clients.

·     In Module One, you will learn about relationship-focused care, role clarification and negotiation in the interprofessional context. You will explore trust and how to build trust and respect for colleagues and other health professionals, the person(s) receiving care, and their care team.

·     In Module Two you will develop the skills to communicate effectively with team members and appreciate the consequences when communication breaks down and is ineffective. This includes the role of communication in patient safety, handovers and transitions of care, health literacy, and communicating with diverse patient and population groups.

·     In Module Three, you explore team functioning, including barriers and enablers, consumer perspectives on team functioning in healthcare, and the psychosocial hazards which impact team functioning.

You will also follow the healthcare journey of a client across the three modules.


Learning outcomes: L05

There are three modules that consist of on-line preparation and face-to-face learning activities. You will work in your team to engage in a range of learning activities to develop your knowledge and skills to help prepare you for working in teams when caring for clients. ·     In Module One, you will learn about relationship-focused care, role clarification and negotiation in the interprofessional context. You will explore trust and how to build trust and respect for colleagues and other health professionals, the person(s) receiving care, and their care team. ·     In Module Two you will develop the skills to communicate effectively with team members and appreciate the consequences when communication breaks down and is ineffective. You will also follow the healthcare journey of a client across the three modules.
